topic.what: Z3-based formal verification engine (COBALT) detecting CWE-190/191/195 arithmetic vulnerability patterns in C/C++ sandbox infrastructure code prior to deployment
topic.how: Libclang AST extraction constructing Z3 bitvector formulas; SAT verdict produces concrete exploitation witness; UNSAT under bounds provides formal safety guarantee
topic.why_matters: Frontier model Mythos sandbox escape exposed that containment infrastructure was never formally verified; capable models can exploit conventional systems weaknesses independent of alignment failures

characteristics.what_it_measures: SAT verdicts confirmed by receiving security teams
characteristics.evidence: No rejected SAT reports among submitted findings; all verdicts acknowledged by NASA F Prime wolfSSL Eclipse security teams
characteristics.context: Production corpus across 4 primary case studies and 1055+ additional findings

characteristics.what_it_measures: SAT verdicts not confirmed as valid vulnerabilities
characteristics.evidence: Guard prototype blocked 2000/2000 unsafe inputs and passed 2000/2000 safe inputs with zero false positives/false negatives
characteristics.context: COBALT-Runtime hybrid shadow execution validation on Mosquitto and NASA cFE patterns

characteristics.what_it_measures: COBALT-Runtime per-check overhead at trust boundary crossings
characteristics.value: 87.2 ns mean, 83.0 ns median, p99 125 ns
characteristics.evidence: Prototype validated against 2000 safe and 2000 unsafe inputs; O(1) integer comparison after offline Z3 derivation
characteristics.context: Minimal guard prototype; production IPC syscall instrumentation is future work

characteristics.evidence: COBALT-Runtime prototype benchmarks on 2 invariant patterns
characteristics.context: Corresponds to 87.2 ns mean latency per check

characteristics.what_it_measures: Z3 solver time for cross-pattern escalation chain verification
characteristics.evidence: COBALT-Graph prototype verified 2-chain escalation paths via single Z3 check()
characteristics.context: Depth-k search beyond 3 chains becomes computationally prohibitive

requires: Explicit input bounds for UNSAT guarantees

novelty: Z3 bitvector formal proof model for CWE-190 arithmetic reachability with concrete SAT witnesses
UNSAT certificate proving overflow elimination via input bound transformation
Four-layer AI containment framework mapping pre-deploy-verification pre-exec-constraints output-control runtime-monitoring
Pattern-class detection of OpenBSD TCP SACK CWE-195 signed-cast overflow without prior knowledge of specific line
Cross-pattern escalation chain verification (COBALT-Graph) composing CWE-190→CWE-125 satisfiability via dataflow bridge
open_problems: COBALT-Δ: differential verification for incremental patch-cycle reanalysis via Z3 push/pop
COBALT-Adapt: CWE-122 heap overflow and CWE-131 malloc size expansion
COBALT-Runtime: production hardening with IPC syscall instrumentation
COBALT-Graph: production-grade compatibility filtering and depth-k enumeration beyond 3 chains
Formal treatment of novel escape vectors outside CWE-190/191/125/476 invariant set
Assumption A-SCOPE non-vacuity: COBALT not engineered post-hoc for Mythos TCP SACK pattern
